displayTitle: Same Part, Two Results: Pinning the Variables

beats.narration: Two inspectors examine the same weld. One finds the crack, one walks past it. The difference isn't eyesight — it's variables.

payload.title: PIN THE VARIABLES
payload.sub: Why two qualified inspectors can pull two different results from one part.

beats.narration: The result rides on four things: light level and direction, surface condition, scan speed and overlap, and you — fatigue and bias.
Watch this shallow crack. Under flood light from above, it disappears. Drop the light to fifteen degrees, and the shadow snaps it into view.

payload.heading: Light angle changes what you see
payload.note: FLOOD LIGHT HIDES · RAKING LIGHT REVEALS

beats.narration: That's why procedures pin the variables in writing — minimum lux verified at the surface, cleaning before you start, a set scan pattern with overlap.

chips.t: Minimum lux, verified at the surface

beats.narration: Here's the rule: a visual test is repeatable only when its variables are pinned in writing. Same part, same call — any qualified inspector, any day.

payload.sub: A visual test is repeatable only when its variables are pinned in writing.
